search_text: NoPe-NeRF: Optimising Neural Radiance Field with No Pose Prior NoPe-NeRF：在无姿势先验的情况下优化神经辐射场 Training a Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) without pre-computed camera poses is challenging. Recent advances in this direction demonstrate the possibility of jointly optimising a NeRF and camera poses in forward-facing scenes. However, these methods still face difficulties during dramatic camera movement. We tackle this challenging problem by incorporating undistorted monocular depth priors. These priors are generated by correcting scale and shift parameters during training, with which we are then able to constrain the relative poses between consecutive frames. This constraint is achieved using our proposed novel loss functions. Experiments on real-world indoor and outdoor scenes show that our method can handle challenging camera trajectories and outperforms existing methods in terms of novel view rendering quality and pose estimation accuracy. Our project page is https://nope-nerf.active.vision. 在没有预先计算相机姿势的情况下训练神经辐射场 (NeRF) 具有挑战性。这个方向的最新进展证明了在前向场景中联合优化 NeRF 和相机姿势的可能性。然而，这些方法在摄像机剧烈移动时仍然面临困难。我们通过结合未失真的单目深度先验来解决这个具有挑战性的问题。这些先验是通过在训练期间校正尺度和移位参数生成的，然后我们就能够约束连续帧之间的相对姿势。这个约束是使用我们提出的新颖的损失函数来实现的。对现实世界室内和室外场景的实验表明，我们的方法可以处理具有挑战性的相机轨迹，并且在新颖的视图渲染质量和姿态估计精度方面优于现有方法。我们的项目页面是 https://nope-nerf.active.vision。 本文提出NoPe-NeRF方法，在无需预计算相机姿态的情况下优化神经辐射场（NeRF），解决剧烈相机运动场景下的重建难题。 Wenjing Bian,Zirui Wang,Kejie Li,Jia-Wang Bian,Victor Adrian Prisacariu cs.CV

authors_text: Zhikang Wang
title: Quantum Control based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 基于深度强化学习的量子控制
comment: submitted as a master's thesis to the Department of Physics, the University of Tokyo
comments: submitted as a master's thesis to the Department of Physics, the University of Tokyo
abstract: In this thesis, we consider two simple but typical control problems and apply deep reinforcement learning to them, i.e., to cool and control a particle which is subject to continuous position measurement in a one-dimensional quadratic potential or in a quartic potential. We compare the performance of reinforcement learning control and conventional control strategies on the two problems, and show that the reinforcement learning achieves a performance comparable to the optimal control for the quadratic case, and outperforms conventional control strategies for the quartic case for which the optimal control strategy is unknown. To our knowledge, this is the first time deep reinforcement learning is applied to quantum control problems in continuous real space. Our research demonstrates that deep reinforcement learning can be used to control a stochastic quantum system in real space effectively as a measurement-feedback closed-loop controller, and our research also shows the ability of AI to discover new control strategies and properties of the quantum systems that are not well understood, and we can gain insights into these problems by learning from the AI, which opens up a new regime for scientific research.
summary: In this thesis, we consider two simple but typical control problems and apply deep reinforcement learning to them, i.e., to cool and control a particle which is subject to continuous position measurement in a one-dimensional quadratic potential or in a quartic potential. We compare the performance of reinforcement learning control and conventional control strategies on the two problems, and show that the reinforcement learning achieves a performance comparable to the optimal control for the quadratic case, and outperforms conventional control strategies for the quartic case for which the optimal control strategy is unknown. To our knowledge, this is the first time deep reinforcement learning is applied to quantum control problems in continuous real space. Our research demonstrates that deep reinforcement learning can be used to control a stochastic quantum system in real space effectively as a measurement-feedback closed-loop controller, and our research also shows the ability of AI to discover new control strategies and properties of the quantum systems that are not well understood, and we can gain insights into these problems by learning from the AI, which opens up a new regime for scientific research.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们考虑两个简单但典型的控制问题，并将深度强化学习应用于它们，即冷却和控制在一维二次势或四次势中进行连续位置测量的粒子。我们在这两个问题上比较了强化学习控制和传统控制策略的性能，结果表明，对于二次情况，强化学习实现了与最优控制相当的性能，并且对于最优控制策略未知的四次情况，优于传统控制策略。据我们所知，这是深度强化学习首次应用于连续实空间中的量子控制问题。我们的研究表明，深度强化学习可以作为测量反馈闭环控制器来有效地控制现实空间中的随机量子系统，我们的研究还表明人工智能有能力发现新的控制策略和尚未被充分理解的量子系统特性，我们可以通过向人工智能学习来深入了解这些问题，这为科学研究开辟了新的领域。

AI.tldr: 本文将深度强化学习应用于量子控制问题，通过比较其与传统控制策略在二次和四次势阱中的性能，验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 传统控制策略在复杂量子系统（如四次势阱）中难以找到最优解，而深度强化学习具有自主探索和优化的潜力，可为量子控制提供新方法。
AI.method: 采用深度强化学习框架，针对连续实空间中受连续位置测量的粒子，在二次势和四次势两种典型势阱中设计控制策略，并与传统方法对比。
AI.result: 深度强化学习在二次势问题中表现接近最优控制，在四次势问题中显著优于传统策略，且首次实现连续实空间量子控制的深度强化学习应用。
AI.conclusion: 深度强化学习能有效控制随机量子系统，揭示量子系统新特性，为科学研究提供基于AI的闭环控制新范式。

authors_text: Zhuoqian Yang,Shikai Li,Wayne Wu,Bo Dai
title: 3DHumanGAN: 3D-Aware Human Image Generation with 3D Pose Mapping
title_zh: 3DHumanGAN：通过 3D 姿势映射生成 3D 感知人体图像
abstract: We present 3DHumanGAN, a 3D-aware generative adversarial network that synthesizes photorealistic images of full-body humans with consistent appearances under different view-angles and body-poses. To tackle the representational and computational challenges in synthesizing the articulated structure of human bodies, we propose a novel generator architecture in which a 2D convolutional backbone is modulated by a 3D pose mapping network. The 3D pose mapping network is formulated as a renderable implicit function conditioned on a posed 3D human mesh. This design has several merits: i) it leverages the strength of 2D GANs to produce high-quality images; ii) it generates consistent images under varying view-angles and poses; iii) the model can incorporate the 3D human prior and enable pose conditioning. Project page: https://3dhumangan.github.io/.
summary: We present 3DHumanGAN, a 3D-aware generative adversarial network that synthesizes photorealistic images of full-body humans with consistent appearances under different view-angles and body-poses. To tackle the representational and computational challenges in synthesizing the articulated structure of human bodies, we propose a novel generator architecture in which a 2D convolutional backbone is modulated by a 3D pose mapping network. The 3D pose mapping network is formulated as a renderable implicit function conditioned on a posed 3D human mesh. This design has several merits: i) it leverages the strength of 2D GANs to produce high-quality images; ii) it generates consistent images under varying view-angles and poses; iii) the model can incorporate the 3D human prior and enable pose conditioning. Project page: https://3dhumangan.github.io/.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 3DHumanGAN，这是一种 3D 感知生成对抗网络，可合成逼真的全身图像，在不同视角和身体姿势下具有一致的外观。为了解决合成人体铰接结构时的表征和计算挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的生成器架构，其中 2D 卷积主干由 3D 姿态映射网络进行调制。 3D 姿势映射网络被公式化为以姿势 3D 人体网格为条件的可渲染隐式函数。这种设计有几个优点：i）它利用 2D GAN 的优势来生成高质量的图像； ii) 在不同的视角和姿势下生成一致的图像； iii) 该模型可以结合 3D 人类先验并启用姿势调节。项目页面：https://3dhumangan.github.io/。

AI.tldr: 本文提出3DHumanGAN，一种基于3D姿态映射的生成对抗网络，用于生成具有不同视角和身体姿势下一致外观的全身人体逼真图像。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在合成人体关节结构时面临表示和计算挑战，难以生成多视角、多姿势下保持外观一致的高质量人体图像。
AI.method: 设计包含2D卷积主干和3D姿态映射网络的生成器架构，后者作为基于姿势3D人体网格的可渲染隐式函数，结合2D GAN优势与3D先验知识。
AI.result: 模型能生成高质量人体图像，在多视角和姿势变化下保持外观一致，并支持姿态条件控制。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了人体图像生成中的3D感知与姿态一致性难题，为未来生成任务提供了新思路。

The 3D pose mapping network is formulated as a renderable implicit function conditioned on a posed 3D human mesh. Project page: https://3dhumangan.github.io/. 我们提出了 3DHumanGAN，这是一种 3D 感知生成对抗网络，可合成逼真的全身图像，在不同视角和身体姿势下具有一致的外观。为了解决合成人体铰接结构时的表征和计算挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的生成器架构，其中 2D 卷积主干由 3D 姿态映射网络进行调制。 3D 姿势映射网络被公式化为以姿势 3D 人体网格为条件的可渲染隐式函数。这种设计有几个优点：i）它利用 2D GAN 的优势来生成高质量的图像； ii) 在不同的视角和姿势下生成一致的图像； iii) 该模型可以结合 3D 人类先验并启用姿势调节。项目页面：https://3dhumangan.github.io/。 本文提出3DHumanGAN，一种基于3D姿态映射的生成对抗网络，用于生成具有不同视角和身体姿势下一致外观的全身人体逼真图像。 Zhuoqian Yang,Shikai Li,Wayne Wu,Bo Dai cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Alaaeldin El-Nouby,Matthew J. Muckley,Karen Ullrich,Ivan Laptev,Jakob Verbeek,Hervé Jégou
title: Image Compression with Product Quantized Masked Image Modeling
title_zh: 使用乘积量化掩模图像建模进行图像压缩
abstract: Recent neural compression methods have been based on the popular hyperprior framework. It relies on Scalar Quantization and offers a very strong compression performance. This contrasts from recent advances in image generation and representation learning, where Vector Quantization is more commonly employed. In this work, we attempt to bring these lines of research closer by revisiting vector quantization for image compression. We build upon the VQ-VAE framework and introduce several modifications. First, we replace the vanilla vector quantizer by a product quantizer. This intermediate solution between vector and scalar quantization allows for a much wider set of rate-distortion points: It implicitly defines high-quality quantizers that would otherwise require intractably large codebooks. Second, inspired by the success of Masked Image Modeling (MIM) in the context of self-supervised learning and generative image models, we propose a novel conditional entropy model which improves entropy coding by modelling the co-dependencies of the quantized latent codes. The resulting PQ-MIM model is surprisingly effective: its compression performance on par with recent hyperprior methods. It also outperforms HiFiC in terms of FID and KID metrics when optimized with perceptual losses (e.g. adversarial). Finally, since PQ-MIM is compatible with image generation frameworks, we show qualitatively that it can operate under a hybrid mode between compression and generation, with no further training or finetuning. As a result, we explore the extreme compression regime where an image is compressed into 200 bytes, i.e., less than a tweet.
summary: Recent neural compression methods have been based on the popular hyperprior framework. It relies on Scalar Quantization and offers a very strong compression performance. This contrasts from recent advances in image generation and representation learning, where Vector Quantization is more commonly employed. In this work, we attempt to bring these lines of research closer by revisiting vector quantization for image compression. We build upon the VQ-VAE framework and introduce several modifications. First, we replace the vanilla vector quantizer by a product quantizer. This intermediate solution between vector and scalar quantization allows for a much wider set of rate-distortion points: It implicitly defines high-quality quantizers that would otherwise require intractably large codebooks. Second, inspired by the success of Masked Image Modeling (MIM) in the context of self-supervised learning and generative image models, we propose a novel conditional entropy model which improves entropy coding by modelling the co-dependencies of the quantized latent codes. The resulting PQ-MIM model is surprisingly effective: its compression performance on par with recent hyperprior methods. It also outperforms HiFiC in terms of FID and KID metrics when optimized with perceptual losses (e.g. adversarial). Finally, since PQ-MIM is compatible with image generation frameworks, we show qualitatively that it can operate under a hybrid mode between compression and generation, with no further training or finetuning. As a result, we explore the extreme compression regime where an image is compressed into 200 bytes, i.e., less than a tweet.
abstract_zh: 最近的神经压缩方法基于流行的超先验框架。它依赖于标量量化并提供非常强大的压缩性能。这与图像生成和表示学习的最新进展形成鲜明对比，其中更普遍采用矢量量化。在这项工作中，我们试图通过重新审视图像压缩的矢量量化来使这些研究更加紧密。我们建立在 VQ-VAE 框架的基础上，并进行了一些修改。首先，我们用乘积量化器替换普通矢量量化器。这种矢量量化和标量量化之间的中间解决方案允许更广泛的率失真点集：它隐式定义了高质量量化器，否则将需要难以处理的大型码本。其次，受掩码图像建模（MIM）在自监督学习和生成图像模型背景下成功的启发，我们提出了一种新颖的条件熵模型，该模型通过对量化潜在代码的相互依赖性进行建模来改进熵编码。由此产生的 PQ-MIM 模型非常有效：其压缩性能与最近的超先验方法相当。当针对感知损失（例如对抗性）进行优化时，它在 FID 和 KID 指标方面也优于 HiFiC。最后，由于 PQ-MIM 与图像生成框架兼容，我们定性地表明它可以在压缩和生成之间的混合模式下运行，无需进一步训练或微调。因此，我们探索了极端压缩机制，将图像压缩为 200 字节，即小于一条推文。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合乘积量化和掩码图像建模的新型图像压缩方法PQ-MIM，旨在提升压缩性能并实现生成与压缩的混合模式。
AI.motivation: 传统超先验框架依赖标量量化虽压缩性能强，但与图像生成领域常用的向量量化方法存在差距。本文试图弥合这一鸿沟，探索更高效的压缩方案。
AI.method: 基于VQ-VAE框架，将普通向量量化替换为乘积量化以扩展率失真点范围，并引入受掩码图像建模启发的条件熵模型，建模量化潜在码的共依赖关系。
AI.result: PQ-MIM在压缩性能上与最新超先验方法相当，使用感知损失优化后在FID/KID指标上优于HiFiC，且可将图像压缩至200字节（小于一条推文）。
AI.conclusion: 乘积量化与掩码图像建模的结合有效提升了压缩效率，同时兼容生成框架，在极低比特率下仍能保持视觉质量，为压缩与生成的统一提供了新思路。

authors_text: Kangfu Mei,Nithin Gopalakrishnan Nair,Vishal M. Patel
title: Bi-Noising Diffusion: Towards Conditional Diffusion Models with Generative Restoration Priors
title_zh: 双噪声扩散：具有生成恢复先验的条件扩散模型
abstract: Conditional diffusion probabilistic models can model the distribution of natural images and can generate diverse and realistic samples based on given conditions. However, oftentimes their results can be unrealistic with observable color shifts and textures. We believe that this issue results from the divergence between the probabilistic distribution learned by the model and the distribution of natural images. The delicate conditions gradually enlarge the divergence during each sampling timestep. To address this issue, we introduce a new method that brings the predicted samples to the training data manifold using a pretrained unconditional diffusion model. The unconditional model acts as a regularizer and reduces the divergence introduced by the conditional model at each sampling step. We perform comprehensive experiments to demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on super-resolution, colorization, turbulence removal, and image-deraining tasks. The improvements obtained by our method suggest that the priors can be incorporated as a general plugin for improving conditional diffusion models.
summary: Conditional diffusion probabilistic models can model the distribution of natural images and can generate diverse and realistic samples based on given conditions. However, oftentimes their results can be unrealistic with observable color shifts and textures. We believe that this issue results from the divergence between the probabilistic distribution learned by the model and the distribution of natural images. The delicate conditions gradually enlarge the divergence during each sampling timestep. To address this issue, we introduce a new method that brings the predicted samples to the training data manifold using a pretrained unconditional diffusion model. The unconditional model acts as a regularizer and reduces the divergence introduced by the conditional model at each sampling step. We perform comprehensive experiments to demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on super-resolution, colorization, turbulence removal, and image-deraining tasks. The improvements obtained by our method suggest that the priors can be incorporated as a general plugin for improving conditional diffusion models.
abstract_zh: 条件扩散概率模型可以对自然图像的分布进行建模，并可以根据给定条件生成多样化且真实的样本。然而，通常他们的结果可能与可观察到的颜色变化和纹理不切实际。我们认为这个问题是由于模型学习的概率分布与自然图像的分布之间的差异造成的。微妙的条件在每个采样时间步长中逐渐扩大散度。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了一种新方法，使用预训练的无条件扩散模型将预测样本引入训练数据流形。无条件模型充当正则化器，并减少条件模型在每个采样步骤引入的发散。我们进行了全面的实验，以证明我们的方法在超分辨率、着色、湍流消除和图像去雨任务方面的有效性。我们的方法获得的改进表明，先验可以作为改进条件扩散模型的通用插件。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Bi-Noising Diffusion的新方法，通过引入预训练无条件扩散模型作为正则化器，减少条件扩散模型在生成过程中的分布差异，从而提升生成图像的真实性。
AI.motivation: 现有条件扩散模型生成的图像存在颜色偏移和纹理不真实问题，主要原因是模型学习的概率分布与自然图像分布存在差异，且条件在采样过程中逐渐放大这种差异。
AI.method: 利用预训练的无条件扩散模型作为正则化器，在每一步采样时将条件模型的预测样本拉回训练数据流形，从而缩小分布差异。
AI.result: 在超分辨率、着色、去湍流和去雨任务中，该方法显著提升了生成效果，验证了其作为通用插件改进条件扩散模型的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入生成修复先验（无条件扩散模型），Bi-Noising Diffusion方法有效缓解了条件扩散模型的分布偏差问题，为提升生成质量提供了通用解决方案。

authors_text: Shintaro Shiba,Yoshimitsu Aoki,Guillermo Gallego
title: A Fast Geometric Regularizer to Mitigate Event Collapse in the Contrast Maximization Framework
title_zh: 用于减轻对比度最大化框架中的事件崩溃的快速几何正则化器
comment: 10 pages, 7 figures, 4 tables. Project page: https://github.com/tub-rip/event collapse
comments: 10 pages, 7 figures, 4 tables. Project page: https://github.com/tub-rip/event collapse
abstract: Event cameras are emerging vision sensors and their advantages are suitable for various applications such as autonomous robots. Contrast maximization (CMax), which provides state-of-the-art accuracy on motion estimation using events, may suffer from an overfitting problem called event collapse. Prior works are computationally expensive or cannot alleviate the overfitting, which undermines the benefits of the CMax framework. We propose a novel, computationally efficient regularizer based on geometric principles to mitigate event collapse. The experiments show that the proposed regularizer achieves state-of-the-art accuracy results, while its reduced computational complexity makes it two to four times faster than previous approaches. To the best of our knowledge, our regularizer is the only effective solution for event collapse without trading off runtime. We hope our work opens the door for future applications that unlocks the advantages of event cameras.
summary: Event cameras are emerging vision sensors and their advantages are suitable for various applications such as autonomous robots. Contrast maximization (CMax), which provides state-of-the-art accuracy on motion estimation using events, may suffer from an overfitting problem called event collapse. Prior works are computationally expensive or cannot alleviate the overfitting, which undermines the benefits of the CMax framework. We propose a novel, computationally efficient regularizer based on geometric principles to mitigate event collapse. The experiments show that the proposed regularizer achieves state-of-the-art accuracy results, while its reduced computational complexity makes it two to four times faster than previous approaches. To the best of our knowledge, our regularizer is the only effective solution for event collapse without trading off runtime. We hope our work opens the door for future applications that unlocks the advantages of event cameras.
abstract_zh: 事件相机是新兴的视觉传感器，其优点适用于自主机器人等各种应用。对比度最大化 (CMax) 使用事件提供最先进的运动估计精度，但可能会遇到称为事件崩溃的过度拟合问题。先前的工作计算量大或者无法缓解过度拟合，这削弱了 CMax 框架的优势。我们提出了一种基于几何原理的新型、计算高效的正则化器，以减轻事件崩溃。实验表明，所提出的正则化器实现了最先进的精度结果，同时其降低的计算复杂性使其比以前的方法快两到四倍。据我们所知，我们的正则化器是唯一有效的事件崩溃解决方案，无需牺牲运行时间。我们希望我们的工作为未来的应用打开大门，释放事件相机的优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于几何原理的高效正则化方法，解决对比最大化框架中的事件崩溃问题。
AI.motivation: 现有对比最大化（CMax）方法在运动估计中易出现事件崩溃过拟合问题，而传统解决方案计算复杂或效果不足，限制了CMax的应用优势。
AI.method: 设计一种计算复杂度低的几何正则化器，通过几何约束缓解事件崩溃，无需牺牲运行效率。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在保持最先进准确率的同时，速度较之前方法提升2-4倍，是首个无需权衡运行时间的有效解决方案。
AI.conclusion: 本工作为事件相机在自主机器人等领域的应用提供了高效可靠的理论支持，推动其优势的进一步发挥。

authors_text: Jianyu Zhang,Léon Bottou
title: Learning useful representations for shifting tasks and distributions
title_zh: 学习用于转移任务和分布的有用表示
comment: Published at ICML 2023. Blog post available at https://www.jianyuzhang.com/blog/rich-representation-learning
comments: Published at ICML 2023. Blog post available at https://www.jianyuzhang.com/blog/rich-representation-learning
abstract: Does the dominant approach to learn representations (as a side effect of optimizing an expected cost for a single training distribution) remain a good approach when we are dealing with multiple distributions? Our thesis is that such scenarios are better served by representations that are richer than those obtained with a single optimization episode. We support this thesis with simple theoretical arguments and with experiments utilizing an apparently naïve ensembling technique: concatenating the representations obtained from multiple training episodes using the same data, model, algorithm, and hyper-parameters, but different random seeds. These independently trained networks perform similarly. Yet, in a number of scenarios involving new distributions, the concatenated representation performs substantially better than an equivalently sized network trained with a single training run. This proves that the representations constructed by multiple training episodes are in fact different. Although their concatenation carries little additional information about the training task under the training distribution, it becomes substantially more informative when tasks or distributions change. Meanwhile, a single training episode is unlikely to yield such a redundant representation because the optimization process has no reason to accumulate features that do not incrementally improve the training performance.
summary: Does the dominant approach to learn representations (as a side effect of optimizing an expected cost for a single training distribution) remain a good approach when we are dealing with multiple distributions? Our thesis is that such scenarios are better served by representations that are richer than those obtained with a single optimization episode. We support this thesis with simple theoretical arguments and with experiments utilizing an apparently naïve ensembling technique: concatenating the representations obtained from multiple training episodes using the same data, model, algorithm, and hyper-parameters, but different random seeds. These independently trained networks perform similarly. Yet, in a number of scenarios involving new distributions, the concatenated representation performs substantially better than an equivalently sized network trained with a single training run. This proves that the representations constructed by multiple training episodes are in fact different. Although their concatenation carries little additional information about the training task under the training distribution, it becomes substantially more informative when tasks or distributions change. Meanwhile, a single training episode is unlikely to yield such a redundant representation because the optimization process has no reason to accumulate features that do not incrementally improve the training performance.
abstract_zh: 当我们处理多个分布时，学习表示的主要方法（作为优化单个训练分布的预期成本的副作用）仍然是一个好方法吗？我们的论点是，与通过单个优化事件获得的表示相比，更丰富的表示可以更好地服务于此类场景。我们通过简单的理论论证和利用明显幼稚的集成技术的实验来支持这篇论文：使用相同的数据、模型、算法和超参数，但使用不同的随机种子，连接从多个训练片段获得的表示。这些独立训练的网络表现相似。然而，在许多涉及新分布的场景中，级联表示的性能明显优于通过单次训练运行训练的同等大小的网络。这证明了多个训练集构建的表示实际上是不同的。尽管它们的串联很少携带有关训练分布下的训练任务的附加信息，但当任务或分布发生变化时，它会变得更加丰富。同时，单个训练集不太可能产生如此冗余的表示，因为优化过程没有理由积累不会逐步提高训练性能的特征。

AI.tldr: 研究在任务和分布变化的情况下，如何学习更有效的表示方法以提升模型适应性。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在单一分布下训练的表示可能无法适应新分布，需探索更丰富的表示方法。
AI.method: 通过多次独立训练相同模型（不同随机种子），拼接各次训练得到的表示以增强表示的多样性。
AI.result: 拼接表示在新分布任务中显著优于单次训练模型，证明多训练回合生成的表示存在差异且更具适应性。
AI.conclusion: 多训练回合生成的冗余表示能更好适应分布变化，单次训练难以获得此类冗余特征。

authors_text: Federica Nenna,Davide Zanardi,Luciano Gamberini
title: Human-centric telerobotics: investigating users' performance and workload via VR-based eye-tracking measures
title_zh: 以人为中心的远程机器人：通过基于 VR 的眼动追踪措施调查用户的表现和工作量
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) is gaining ground in the robotics and teleoperation industry, opening new prospects as a novel computerized methodology to make humans interact with robots. In contrast with more conventional button-based teleoperations, VR allows users to use their physical movements to drive robotic systems in the virtual environment. The latest VR devices are also equipped with integrated eye-tracking, which constitutes an exceptional opportunity for monitoring users' workload online. However, such devices are fairly recent, and human factors have been consistently marginalized so far in telerobotics research. We thus covered these aspects by analyzing extensive behavioral data generated by 24 participants driving a simulated industrial robot in VR through a pick-and-place task. Users drove the robot via button-based and action-based controls and under low (single-task) and high (dual-task) mental demands. We collected self-reports, performance and eye-tracking data. Specifically, we asked i) how the interactive features of VR affect users' performance and workload, and additionally tested ii) the sensibility of diverse eye parameters in monitoring users' vigilance and workload throughout the task. Users performed faster and more accurately, while also showing a lower mental workload, when using an action-based VR control. Among the eye parameters, pupil size was the most resilient indicator of workload, as it was highly correlated with the self-reports and was not affected by the user's degree of physical motion in VR. Our results thus bring a fresh human-centric overview of human-robot interactions in VR, and systematically demonstrate the potential of VR devices for monitoring human factors in telerobotics contexts.
summary: Virtual Reality (VR) is gaining ground in the robotics and teleoperation industry, opening new prospects as a novel computerized methodology to make humans interact with robots. In contrast with more conventional button-based teleoperations, VR allows users to use their physical movements to drive robotic systems in the virtual environment. The latest VR devices are also equipped with integrated eye-tracking, which constitutes an exceptional opportunity for monitoring users' workload online. However, such devices are fairly recent, and human factors have been consistently marginalized so far in telerobotics research. We thus covered these aspects by analyzing extensive behavioral data generated by 24 participants driving a simulated industrial robot in VR through a pick-and-place task. Users drove the robot via button-based and action-based controls and under low (single-task) and high (dual-task) mental demands. We collected self-reports, performance and eye-tracking data. Specifically, we asked i) how the interactive features of VR affect users' performance and workload, and additionally tested ii) the sensibility of diverse eye parameters in monitoring users' vigilance and workload throughout the task. Users performed faster and more accurately, while also showing a lower mental workload, when using an action-based VR control. Among the eye parameters, pupil size was the most resilient indicator of workload, as it was highly correlated with the self-reports and was not affected by the user's degree of physical motion in VR. Our results thus bring a fresh human-centric overview of human-robot interactions in VR, and systematically demonstrate the potential of VR devices for monitoring human factors in telerobotics contexts.
abstract_zh: 虚拟现实（VR）在机器人和远程操作行业中正在取得进展，作为一种使人类与机器人互动的新型计算机化方法开辟了新的前景。与更传统的基于按钮的远程操作相比，VR 允许用户使用他们的身体动作来驱动虚拟环境中的机器人系统。最新的 VR 设备还配备了集成眼动追踪功能，这为在线监控用户的工作量提供了绝佳的机会。然而，此类设备是最近才出现的，迄今为止，人为因素在远程机器人研究中一直被边缘化。因此，我们通过分析 24 名参与者在 VR 中驾驶模拟工业机器人执行拾放任务所生成的大量行为数据来涵盖这些方面。用户通过基于按钮和基于动作的控制以及低（单任务）和高（双任务）心理需求来驾驶机器人。我们收集了自我报告、表现和眼动追踪数据。具体来说，我们询问了 i) VR 的交互功能如何影响用户的表现和工作量，并另外测试了 ii) 不同眼睛参数在整个任务中监控用户的警惕性和工作量的敏感性。当使用基于动作的 VR 控制时，用户表现得更快、更准确，同时也表现出较低的脑力负荷。在眼睛参数中，瞳孔大小是最具弹性的工作负荷指标，因为它与自我报告高度相关，并且不受用户在 VR 中身体运动程度的影响。因此，我们的研究结果为 VR 中的人机交互带来了以人为中心的全新概述，并系统地展示了 VR 设备在远程机器人环境中监控人为因素的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文通过VR眼动追踪技术研究人类在远程机器人操作中的表现和工作负荷，对比了基于按钮和动作控制方式的差异。
AI.motivation: VR设备在机器人领域应用增多，但传统研究忽视了人类因素。本文旨在通过眼动追踪技术填补这一空白，探索VR交互对用户表现和工作负荷的影响。
AI.method: 24名参与者通过VR操作模拟工业机器人完成抓取任务，采用按钮式和动作式控制方式，并设置单任务和双任务条件，结合自评、表现数据和眼动参数进行分析。
AI.result: 动作式控制使用户操作更快更准确且工作负荷更低；瞳孔大小是监测工作负荷最可靠的指标，与自评高度相关且不受VR中身体运动影响。
AI.conclusion: 研究从以人为本的角度揭示了VR中人机交互的新视角，系统验证了VR设备在远程机器人场景中监测人类因素的潜力。

ai_content.tldr: 本文通过VR眼动追踪技术研究人类在远程机器人操作中的表现和工作负荷，对比了基于按钮和动作控制方式的差异。
ai_content.motivation: VR设备在机器人领域应用增多，但传统研究忽视了人类因素。本文旨在通过眼动追踪技术填补这一空白，探索VR交互对用户表现和工作负荷的影响。
ai_content.method: 24名参与者通过VR操作模拟工业机器人完成抓取任务，采用按钮式和动作式控制方式，并设置单任务和双任务条件，结合自评、表现数据和眼动参数进行分析。
ai_content.result: 动作式控制使用户操作更快更准确且工作负荷更低；瞳孔大小是监测工作负荷最可靠的指标，与自评高度相关且不受VR中身体运动影响。
ai_content.conclusion: 研究从以人为本的角度揭示了VR中人机交互的新视角，系统验证了VR设备在远程机器人场景中监测人类因素的潜力。

ai_content_json.tldr: 本文通过VR眼动追踪技术研究人类在远程机器人操作中的表现和工作负荷，对比了基于按钮和动作控制方式的差异。

search_text: Human-centric telerobotics: investigating users' performance and workload via VR-based eye-tracking measures 以人为中心的远程机器人：通过基于 VR 的眼动追踪措施调查用户的表现和工作量 Virtual Reality (VR) is gaining ground in the robotics and teleoperation industry, opening new prospects as a novel computerized methodology to make humans interact with robots. In contrast with more conventional button-based teleoperations, VR allows users to use their physical movements to drive robotic systems in the virtual environment. The latest VR devices are also equipped with integrated eye-tracking, which constitutes an exceptional opportunity for monitoring users' workload online. However, such devices are fairly recent, and human factors have been consistently marginalized so far in telerobotics research. We thus covered these aspects by analyzing extensive behavioral data generated by 24 participants driving a simulated industrial robot in VR through a pick-and-place task. Users drove the robot via button-based and action-based controls and under low (single-task) and high (dual-task) mental demands. We collected self-reports, performance and eye-tracking data. Specifically, we asked i) how the interactive features of VR affect users' performance and workload, and additionally tested ii) the sensibility of diverse eye parameters in monitoring users' vigilance and workload throughout the task. Users performed faster and more accurately, while also showing a lower mental workload, when using an action-based VR control. Among the eye parameters, pupil size was the most resilient indicator of workload, as it was highly correlated with the self-reports and was not affected by the user's degree of physical motion in VR. Our results thus bring a fresh human-centric overview of human-robot interactions in VR, and systematically demonstrate the potential of VR devices for monitoring human factors in telerobotics contexts. 虚拟现实（VR）在机器人和远程操作行业中正在取得进展，作为一种使人类与机器人互动的新型计算机化方法开辟了新的前景。与更传统的基于按钮的远程操作相比，VR 允许用户使用他们的身体动作来驱动虚拟环境中的机器人系统。最新的 VR 设备还配备了集成眼动追踪功能，这为在线监控用户的工作量提供了绝佳的机会。然而，此类设备是最近才出现的，迄今为止，人为因素在远程机器人研究中一直被边缘化。因此，我们通过分析 24 名参与者在 VR 中驾驶模拟工业机器人执行拾放任务所生成的大量行为数据来涵盖这些方面。用户通过基于按钮和基于动作的控制以及低（单任务）和高（双任务）心理需求来驾驶机器人。我们收集了自我报告、表现和眼动追踪数据。具体来说，我们询问了 i) VR 的交互功能如何影响用户的表现和工作量，并另外测试了 ii) 不同眼睛参数在整个任务中监控用户的警惕性和工作量的敏感性。当使用基于动作的 VR 控制时，用户表现得更快、更准确，同时也表现出较低的脑力负荷。在眼睛参数中，瞳孔大小是最具弹性的工作负荷指标，因为它与自我报告高度相关，并且不受用户在 VR 中身体运动程度的影响。因此，我们的研究结果为 VR 中的人机交互带来了以人为中心的全新概述，并系统地展示了 VR 设备在远程机器人环境中监控人为因素的潜力。 本文通过VR眼动追踪技术研究人类在远程机器人操作中的表现和工作负荷，对比了基于按钮和动作控制方式的差异。 Federica Nenna,Davide Zanardi,Luciano Gamberini cs.HC

authors_text: Liangbin Xie,Xintao Wang,Shuwei Shi,Jinjin Gu,Chao Dong,Ying Shan
title: Mitigating Artifacts in Real-World Video Super-Resolution Models
title_zh: 减轻现实世界视频超分辨率模型中的伪影
comment: Accepted by AAAI 2023. Codes will be available at https://github.com/TencentARC/FastRealVSR
comments: Accepted by AAAI 2023. Codes will be available at https://github.com/TencentARC/FastRealVSR
abstract: The recurrent structure is a prevalent framework for the task of video super-resolution, which models the temporal dependency between frames via hidden states. When applied to real-world scenarios with unknown and complex degradations, hidden states tend to contain unpleasant artifacts and propagate them to restored frames. In this circumstance, our analyses show that such artifacts can be largely alleviated when the hidden state is replaced with a cleaner counterpart. Based on the observations, we propose a Hidden State Attention (HSA) module to mitigate artifacts in real-world video super-resolution. Specifically, we first adopt various cheap filters to produce a hidden state pool. For example, Gaussian blur filters are for smoothing artifacts while sharpening filters are for enhancing details. To aggregate a new hidden state that contains fewer artifacts from the hidden state pool, we devise a Selective Cross Attention (SCA) module, in which the attention between input features and each hidden state is calculated. Equipped with HSA, our proposed method, namely FastRealVSR, is able to achieve 2x speedup while obtaining better performance than Real-BasicVSR. Codes will be available at https://github.com/TencentARC/FastRealVSR
summary: The recurrent structure is a prevalent framework for the task of video super-resolution, which models the temporal dependency between frames via hidden states. When applied to real-world scenarios with unknown and complex degradations, hidden states tend to contain unpleasant artifacts and propagate them to restored frames. In this circumstance, our analyses show that such artifacts can be largely alleviated when the hidden state is replaced with a cleaner counterpart. Based on the observations, we propose a Hidden State Attention (HSA) module to mitigate artifacts in real-world video super-resolution. Specifically, we first adopt various cheap filters to produce a hidden state pool. For example, Gaussian blur filters are for smoothing artifacts while sharpening filters are for enhancing details. To aggregate a new hidden state that contains fewer artifacts from the hidden state pool, we devise a Selective Cross Attention (SCA) module, in which the attention between input features and each hidden state is calculated. Equipped with HSA, our proposed method, namely FastRealVSR, is able to achieve 2x speedup while obtaining better performance than Real-BasicVSR. Codes will be available at https://github.com/TencentARC/FastRealVSR
abstract_zh: 循环结构是视频超分辨率任务的流行框架，它通过隐藏状态对帧之间的时间依赖性进行建模。当应用于具有未知和复杂退化的现实场景时，隐藏状态往往包含令人不快的伪影并将其传播到恢复的帧。在这种情况下，我们的分析表明，当隐藏状态被替换为更清晰的对应状态时，此类伪影可以在很大程度上得到缓解。根据观察结果，我们提出了一个隐藏状态注意（HSA）模块来减轻现实世界视频超分辨率中的伪影。具体来说，我们首先采用各种廉价的过滤器来生成隐藏状态池。例如，高斯模糊滤波器用于平滑伪影，而锐化滤波器用于增强细节。为了聚合一个包含来自隐藏状态池的较少工件的新隐藏状态，我们设计了一个选择性交叉注意力（SCA）模块，其中计算输入特征和每个隐藏状态之间的注意力。配备 HSA，我们提出的方法，即 FastRealVSR，能够实现 2 倍的加速，同时获得比 Real-BasicVSR 更好的性能。代码可在 https://github.com/TencentARC/FastRealVSR 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于真实世界视频超分辨率模型的隐藏状态注意力模块（HSA），以减少视频超分辨率过程中的伪影问题。
AI.motivation: 现有视频超分辨率模型在处理真实世界复杂退化场景时，由于隐藏状态中包含的伪影会传播到恢复帧，导致重建质量下降。
AI.method: 通过引入HSA模块，采用多种廉价滤波器生成隐藏状态池，并设计选择性交叉注意力（SCA）模块从池中聚合更干净的隐藏状态。
AI.result: 所提出的FastRealVSR方法在保持更高性能的同时实现了2倍加速，优于Real-BasicVSR模型。
AI.conclusion: HSA模块能有效缓解真实场景下视频超分辨率的伪影问题，同时提升计算效率。

authors_text: Zhitong Gao,Yucong Chen,Chuyu Zhang,Xuming He
title: Modeling Multimodal Aleatoric Uncertainty in Segmentation with Mixture of Stochastic Experts
title_zh: 混合随机专家对分割中的多峰任意不确定性进行建模
abstract: Equipping predicted segmentation with calibrated uncertainty is essential for safety-critical applications. In this work, we focus on capturing the data-inherent uncertainty (aka aleatoric uncertainty) in segmentation, typically when ambiguities exist in input images. Due to the high-dimensional output space and potential multiple modes in segmenting ambiguous images, it remains challenging to predict well-calibrated uncertainty for segmentation. To tackle this problem, we propose a novel mixture of stochastic experts (MoSE) model, where each expert network estimates a distinct mode of the aleatoric uncertainty and a gating network predicts the probabilities of an input image being segmented in those modes. This yields an efficient two-level uncertainty representation. To learn the model, we develop a Wasserstein-like loss that directly minimizes the distribution distance between the MoSE and ground truth annotations. The loss can easily integrate traditional segmentation quality measures and be efficiently optimized via constraint relaxation. We validate our method on the LIDC-IDRI dataset and a modified multimodal Cityscapes dataset. Results demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art or competitive performance on all metrics.
summary: Equipping predicted segmentation with calibrated uncertainty is essential for safety-critical applications. In this work, we focus on capturing the data-inherent uncertainty (aka aleatoric uncertainty) in segmentation, typically when ambiguities exist in input images. Due to the high-dimensional output space and potential multiple modes in segmenting ambiguous images, it remains challenging to predict well-calibrated uncertainty for segmentation. To tackle this problem, we propose a novel mixture of stochastic experts (MoSE) model, where each expert network estimates a distinct mode of the aleatoric uncertainty and a gating network predicts the probabilities of an input image being segmented in those modes. This yields an efficient two-level uncertainty representation. To learn the model, we develop a Wasserstein-like loss that directly minimizes the distribution distance between the MoSE and ground truth annotations. The loss can easily integrate traditional segmentation quality measures and be efficiently optimized via constraint relaxation. We validate our method on the LIDC-IDRI dataset and a modified multimodal Cityscapes dataset. Results demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art or competitive performance on all metrics.
abstract_zh: 为预测的分割配备校准的不确定性对于安全关键型应用至关重要。在这项工作中，我们专注于捕获分割中数据固有的不确定性（也称为任意不确定性），通常是在输入图像中存在模糊性时。由于高维输出空间和分割模糊图像时潜在的多种模式，预测经过良好校准的分割不确定性仍然具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的随机专家混合（MoSE）模型，其中每个专家网络估计任意不确定性的不同模式，而门控网络预测输入图像在这些模式下被分割的概率。这产生了有效的两级不确定性表示。为了学习该模型，我们开发了一种类似 Wasserstein 的损失，可以直接最小化 MoSE 和真实注释之间的分布距离。该损失可以轻松地集成传统的分割质量度量，并通过约束松弛进行有效优化。我们在 LIDC-IDRI 数据集和修改后的多模式 Cityscapes 数据集上验证了我们的方法。结果表明，我们的方法在所有指标上都达到了最先进的或有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出一种混合随机专家模型（MoSE）来建模分割任务中的多模态随机不确定性，以提高安全关键应用的可靠性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以处理分割中的多模态不确定性，导致在存在输入模糊时预测的不确定性不准确，影响安全关键应用的可靠性。
AI.method: 提出混合随机专家（MoSE）模型，包含多个专家网络估计不同不确定性模式和门控网络预测模式概率，结合类似Wasserstein的损失函数优化模型。
AI.result: 在LIDC-IDRI和多模态Cityscapes数据集上验证，各项指标表现达到或优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: MoSE模型有效解决了分割任务中多模态随机不确定性的建模问题，提升了分割结果的可靠性。

tldr: 提出一种混合随机专家模型（MoSE）来建模分割任务中的多模态随机不确定性，以提高安全关键应用的可靠性。
motivation: 现有方法难以处理分割中的多模态不确定性，导致在存在输入模糊时预测的不确定性不准确，影响安全关键应用的可靠性。
method: 提出混合随机专家（MoSE）模型，包含多个专家网络估计不同不确定性模式和门控网络预测模式概率，结合类似Wasserstein的损失函数优化模型。
result: 在LIDC-IDRI和多模态Cityscapes数据集上验证，各项指标表现达到或优于现有最先进方法。
conclusion: MoSE模型有效解决了分割任务中多模态随机不确定性的建模问题，提升了分割结果的可靠性。
search_text: Modeling Multimodal Aleatoric Uncertainty in Segmentation with Mixture of Stochastic Experts 混合随机专家对分割中的多峰任意不确定性进行建模 Equipping predicted segmentation with calibrated uncertainty is essential for safety-critical applications. In this work, we focus on capturing the data-inherent uncertainty (aka aleatoric uncertainty) in segmentation, typically when ambiguities exist in input images. Due to the high-dimensional output space and potential multiple modes in segmenting ambiguous images, it remains challenging to predict well-calibrated uncertainty for segmentation. To tackle this problem, we propose a novel mixture of stochastic experts (MoSE) model, where each expert network estimates a distinct mode of the aleatoric uncertainty and a gating network predicts the probabilities of an input image being segmented in those modes. This yields an efficient two-level uncertainty representation. To learn the model, we develop a Wasserstein-like loss that directly minimizes the distribution distance between the MoSE and ground truth annotations. The loss can easily integrate traditional segmentation quality measures and be efficiently optimized via constraint relaxation. We validate our method on the LIDC-IDRI dataset and a modified multimodal Cityscapes dataset. Results demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art or competitive performance on all metrics. 为预测的分割配备校准的不确定性对于安全关键型应用至关重要。在这项工作中，我们专注于捕获分割中数据固有的不确定性（也称为任意不确定性），通常是在输入图像中存在模糊性时。由于高维输出空间和分割模糊图像时潜在的多种模式，预测经过良好校准的分割不确定性仍然具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的随机专家混合（MoSE）模型，其中每个专家网络估计任意不确定性的不同模式，而门控网络预测输入图像在这些模式下被分割的概率。这产生了有效的两级不确定性表示。为了学习该模型，我们开发了一种类似 Wasserstein 的损失，可以直接最小化 MoSE 和真实注释之间的分布距离。该损失可以轻松地集成传统的分割质量度量，并通过约束松弛进行有效优化。我们在 LIDC-IDRI 数据集和修改后的多模式 Cityscapes 数据集上验证了我们的方法。结果表明，我们的方法在所有指标上都达到了最先进的或有竞争力的性能。 提出一种混合随机专家模型（MoSE）来建模分割任务中的多模态随机不确定性，以提高安全关键应用的可靠性。 Zhitong Gao,Yucong Chen,Chuyu Zhang,Xuming He cs.CV

authors_text: Darius Petermann,Gordon Wichern,Aswin Shanmugam Subramanian,Zhong-Qiu Wang,Jonathan Le Roux
title: Tackling the Cocktail Fork Problem for Separation and Transcription of Real-World Soundtracks
title_zh: 解决现实世界音轨分离和转录的鸡尾酒叉问题
comment: Submitted to IEEE TASLP (In review), 13 pages, 6 figures
comments: Submitted to IEEE TASLP (In review), 13 pages, 6 figures
abstract: Emulating the human ability to solve the cocktail party problem, i.e., focus on a source of interest in a complex acoustic scene, is a long standing goal of audio source separation research. Much of this research investigates separating speech from noise, speech from speech, musical instruments from each other, or sound events from each other. In this paper, we focus on the cocktail fork problem, which takes a three-pronged approach to source separation by separating an audio mixture such as a movie soundtrack or podcast into the three broad categories of speech, music, and sound effects (SFX - understood to include ambient noise and natural sound events). We benchmark the performance of several deep learning-based source separation models on this task and evaluate them with respect to simple objective measures such as signal-to-distortion ratio (SDR) as well as objective metrics that better correlate with human perception. Furthermore, we thoroughly evaluate how source separation can influence downstream transcription tasks. First, we investigate the task of activity detection on the three sources as a way to both further improve source separation and perform transcription. We formulate the transcription tasks as speech recognition for speech and audio tagging for music and SFX. We observe that, while the use of source separation estimates improves transcription performance in comparison to the original soundtrack, performance is still sub-optimal due to artifacts introduced by the separation process. Therefore, we thoroughly investigate how remixing of the three separated source stems at various relative levels can reduce artifacts and consequently improve the transcription performance. We find that remixing music and SFX interferences at a target SNR of 17.5 dB reduces speech recognition word error rate, and similar impact from remixing is observed for tagging music and SFX content.
summary: Emulating the human ability to solve the cocktail party problem, i.e., focus on a source of interest in a complex acoustic scene, is a long standing goal of audio source separation research. Much of this research investigates separating speech from noise, speech from speech, musical instruments from each other, or sound events from each other. In this paper, we focus on the cocktail fork problem, which takes a three-pronged approach to source separation by separating an audio mixture such as a movie soundtrack or podcast into the three broad categories of speech, music, and sound effects (SFX - understood to include ambient noise and natural sound events). We benchmark the performance of several deep learning-based source separation models on this task and evaluate them with respect to simple objective measures such as signal-to-distortion ratio (SDR) as well as objective metrics that better correlate with human perception. Furthermore, we thoroughly evaluate how source separation can influence downstream transcription tasks. First, we investigate the task of activity detection on the three sources as a way to both further improve source separation and perform transcription. We formulate the transcription tasks as speech recognition for speech and audio tagging for music and SFX. We observe that, while the use of source separation estimates improves transcription performance in comparison to the original soundtrack, performance is still sub-optimal due to artifacts introduced by the separation process. Therefore, we thoroughly investigate how remixing of the three separated source stems at various relative levels can reduce artifacts and consequently improve the transcription performance. We find that remixing music and SFX interferences at a target SNR of 17.5 dB reduces speech recognition word error rate, and similar impact from remixing is observed for tagging music and SFX content.
abstract_zh: 模拟人类解决鸡尾酒会问题的能力，即关注复杂声学场景中的兴趣源，是音频源分离研究的长期目标。这项研究的大部分内容是研究如何将语音与噪声、语音与语音、乐器彼此或声音事件彼此分开。在本文中，我们重点关注鸡尾酒叉问题，该问题采用三管齐下的方法进行源分离，将电影配乐或播客等音频混合物分为语音、音乐和音效（SFX - 理解为包括环境噪声和自然声音事件）三大类。我们在此任务上对几种基于深度学习的源分离模型的性能进行了基准测试，并根据简单的客观指标（例如信号失真比（SDR））以及与人类感知更好相关的客观指标来评估它们。此外，我们彻底评估了源分离如何影响下游转录任务。首先，我们研究了三个源的活动检测任务，作为进一步改进源分离和执行转录的一种方法。我们将转录任务制定为针对语音的语音识别以及针对音乐和 SFX 的音频标记。我们观察到，虽然与原始音轨相比，使用源分离估计提高了转录性能，但由于分离过程引入的伪影，性能仍然次优。因此，我们深入研究了三个分离的源词干在不同相对水平上的重新混合如何减少伪影，从而提高转录性能。我们发现，以 17.5 dB 的目标 SNR 重新混合音乐和 SFX 干扰可降低语音识别单词错误率，并且对于标记音乐和 SFX 内容，也可以观察到重新混合产生的类似影响。

AI.tldr: 本文针对现实世界声轨的分离与转录问题，提出将音频混合物分为语音、音乐和音效三类的解决方案，并评估源分离对下游转录任务的影响。
AI.motivation: 传统音频分离研究多聚焦于语音与噪声或乐器分离，但实际场景中需区分语音、音乐及音效三类内容，以提升复杂声学场景下的转录准确性。
AI.method: 基于深度学习模型实现三类源分离，结合客观指标（如SDR）与感知相关评估；通过活动检测优化分离结果，并将转录任务拆解为语音识别与音频标签，最终通过调整分离源的混合比例（如设定目标SNR）减少伪影。
AI.result: 源分离显著提升转录性能，但存在分离伪影；在17.5dB目标SNR下混合音乐与音效可降低语音识别词错误率，类似优化策略对音效标签亦有效。
AI.conclusion: 源分离可有效辅助转录，但需通过混合策略缓解伪影影响，未来研究可进一步探索混合参数与任务性能的动态适配关系。

authors_text: Joakim Tutt,Olga Taran,Roman Chaban,Brian Pulfer,Yury Belousov,Taras Holotyak,Slava Voloshynovskiy
title: Mathematical model of printing-imaging channel for blind detection of fake copy detection patterns
title_zh: 仿冒检测图案盲检测的印刷成像通道数学模型
comment: Paper accepted at the IEEE International Workshop on Information Forensics and Security (WIFS) 2022
comments: Paper accepted at the IEEE International Workshop on Information Forensics and Security (WIFS) 2022
abstract: Nowadays, copy detection patterns (CDP) appear as a very promising anti-counterfeiting technology for physical object protection. However, the advent of deep learning as a powerful attacking tool has shown that the general authentication schemes are unable to compete and fail against such attacks. In this paper, we propose a new mathematical model of printing-imaging channel for the authentication of CDP together with a new detection scheme based on it. The results show that even deep learning created copy fakes unknown at the training stage can be reliably authenticated based on the proposed approach and using only digital references of CDP during authentication.
summary: Nowadays, copy detection patterns (CDP) appear as a very promising anti-counterfeiting technology for physical object protection. However, the advent of deep learning as a powerful attacking tool has shown that the general authentication schemes are unable to compete and fail against such attacks. In this paper, we propose a new mathematical model of printing-imaging channel for the authentication of CDP together with a new detection scheme based on it. The results show that even deep learning created copy fakes unknown at the training stage can be reliably authenticated based on the proposed approach and using only digital references of CDP during authentication.
abstract_zh: 如今，复制检测模式（CDP）作为一种非常有前途的物理对象保护防伪技术而出现。然而，深度学习作为一种强大的攻击工具的出现表明，一般的身份验证方案无法与此类攻击竞争并失败。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于 CDP 认证的新印刷成像通道数学模型以及基于该模型的新检测方案。结果表明，即使深度学习在训练阶段创建了未知的复制品，也可以根据所提出的方法进行可靠的验证，并且在验证过程中仅使用 CDP 的数字参考。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种印刷成像通道的数学模型及基于该模型的检测方案，用于盲检测伪造的复制检测模式（CDP）。
AI.motivation: 现有认证方案难以应对深度学习生成的未知伪造攻击，需开发新型防伪技术以提升安全性。
AI.method: 构建印刷成像通道的数学模型，并设计基于数字参考CDP的检测算法，无需训练阶段即可认证伪造品。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能可靠识别深度学习生成的未知伪造CDP，仅需数字参考即可完成认证。
AI.conclusion: 所提模型与检测方案有效应对深度学习攻击，为物理对象防伪提供了更安全的解决方案。

motivation: 现有认证方案难以应对深度学习生成的未知伪造攻击，需开发新型防伪技术以提升安全性。
method: 构建印刷成像通道的数学模型，并设计基于数字参考CDP的检测算法，无需训练阶段即可认证伪造品。
result: 实验表明，该方法能可靠识别深度学习生成的未知伪造CDP，仅需数字参考即可完成认证。
conclusion: 所提模型与检测方案有效应对深度学习攻击，为物理对象防伪提供了更安全的解决方案。
search_text: Mathematical model of printing-imaging channel for blind detection of fake copy detection patterns 仿冒检测图案盲检测的印刷成像通道数学模型 Nowadays, copy detection patterns (CDP) appear as a very promising anti-counterfeiting technology for physical object protection. However, the advent of deep learning as a powerful attacking tool has shown that the general authentication schemes are unable to compete and fail against such attacks. In this paper, we propose a new mathematical model of printing-imaging channel for the authentication of CDP together with a new detection scheme based on it. The results show that even deep learning created copy fakes unknown at the training stage can be reliably authenticated based on the proposed approach and using only digital references of CDP during authentication. 如今，复制检测模式（CDP）作为一种非常有前途的物理对象保护防伪技术而出现。然而，深度学习作为一种强大的攻击工具的出现表明，一般的身份验证方案无法与此类攻击竞争并失败。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于 CDP 认证的新印刷成像通道数学模型以及基于该模型的新检测方案。结果表明，即使深度学习在训练阶段创建了未知的复制品，也可以根据所提出的方法进行可靠的验证，并且在验证过程中仅使用 CDP 的数字参考。 本文提出一种印刷成像通道的数学模型及基于该模型的检测方案，用于盲检测伪造的复制检测模式（CDP）。 Joakim Tutt,Olga Taran,Roman Chaban,Brian Pulfer,Yury Belousov,Taras Holotyak,Slava Voloshynovskiy cs.CV,cs.CR

authors_text: Philipp Markert,Leona Lassak,Maximilian Golla,Markus Dürmuth
title: Understanding Users' Interaction with Login Notifications
title_zh: 了解用户与登录通知的交互
abstract: Login notifications intend to inform users about sign-ins and help them protect their accounts from unauthorized access. Notifications are usually sent if a login deviates from previous ones, potentially indicating malicious activity. They contain information like the location, date, time, and device used to sign in. Users are challenged to verify whether they recognize the login (because it was them or someone they know) or to protect their account from unwanted access. In a user study, we explore users' comprehension, reactions, and expectations of login notifications. We utilize two treatments to measure users' behavior in response to notifications sent for a login they initiated or based on a malicious actor relying on statistical sign-in information. We find that users identify legitimate logins but need more support to halt malicious sign-ins. We discuss the identified problems and give recommendations for service providers to ensure usable and secure logins for everyone.
summary: Login notifications intend to inform users about sign-ins and help them protect their accounts from unauthorized access. Notifications are usually sent if a login deviates from previous ones, potentially indicating malicious activity. They contain information like the location, date, time, and device used to sign in. Users are challenged to verify whether they recognize the login (because it was them or someone they know) or to protect their account from unwanted access. In a user study, we explore users' comprehension, reactions, and expectations of login notifications. We utilize two treatments to measure users' behavior in response to notifications sent for a login they initiated or based on a malicious actor relying on statistical sign-in information. We find that users identify legitimate logins but need more support to halt malicious sign-ins. We discuss the identified problems and give recommendations for service providers to ensure usable and secure logins for everyone.
abstract_zh: Login notifications intend to inform users about sign-ins and help them protect their accounts from unauthorized access. Notifications are usually sent if a login deviates from previous ones, potentially indicating malicious activity.它们包含用于登录的位置、日期、时间和设备等信息。用户需要验证他们是否识别登录信息（因为是他们或他们认识的人）或保护他们的帐户免受不必要的访问。 In a user study, we explore users' comprehension, reactions, and expectations of login notifications.我们利用两种处理方法来测量用户的行为，以响应针对他们发起的登录发送的通知或基于依赖统计登录信息的恶意行为者。 We find that users identify legitimate logins but need more support to halt malicious sign-ins. We discuss the identified problems and give recommendations for service providers to ensure usable and secure logins for everyone.

AI.tldr: 本论文旨在研究用户对登录通知的互动方式，评估其对正常及异常登录行为的识别能力，并提出改进通知系统的建议。
AI.motivation: 现有登录通知系统可能无法有效帮助用户识别和应对恶意登录行为，需通过研究用户交互方式来优化安全性和可用性。
AI.method: 通过用户实验，采用两种处理条件（用户自主登录与模拟恶意登录）观察用户对登录通知的反应及应对行为。
AI.result: 研究发现用户能有效识别正常登录，但在应对潜在恶意登录时缺乏足够支持，需进一步协助以增强账户保护。
AI.conclusion: 用户需更直观的登录通知设计及明确的操作指导，服务提供商应优化通知机制以提升安全性和用户体验。

authors_text: John Lambert,James Hays
title: Trust, but Verify: Cross-Modality Fusion for HD Map Change Detection
title_zh: 信任但验证：用于高精地图变化检测的跨模态融合
comment: NeurIPS 2021, Track on Datasets and Benchmarks. Project page: https://tbv-dataset.github.io/
comments: NeurIPS 2021, Track on Datasets and Benchmarks. Project page: https://tbv-dataset.github.io/
abstract: High-definition (HD) map change detection is the task of determining when sensor data and map data are no longer in agreement with one another due to real-world changes. We collect the first dataset for the task, which we entitle the Trust, but Verify (TbV) dataset, by mining thousands of hours of data from over 9 months of autonomous vehicle fleet operations. We present learning-based formulations for solving the problem in the bird's eye view and ego-view. Because real map changes are infrequent and vector maps are easy to synthetically manipulate, we lean on simulated data to train our model. Perhaps surprisingly, we show that such models can generalize to real world distributions. The dataset, consisting of maps and logs collected in six North American cities, is one of the largest AV datasets to date with more than 7.8 million images. We make the data available to the public at https://www.argoverse.org/av2.html#mapchange-link, along with code and models at https://github.com/johnwlambert/tbv under the the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license.
summary: High-definition (HD) map change detection is the task of determining when sensor data and map data are no longer in agreement with one another due to real-world changes. We collect the first dataset for the task, which we entitle the Trust, but Verify (TbV) dataset, by mining thousands of hours of data from over 9 months of autonomous vehicle fleet operations. We present learning-based formulations for solving the problem in the bird's eye view and ego-view. Because real map changes are infrequent and vector maps are easy to synthetically manipulate, we lean on simulated data to train our model. Perhaps surprisingly, we show that such models can generalize to real world distributions. The dataset, consisting of maps and logs collected in six North American cities, is one of the largest AV datasets to date with more than 7.8 million images. We make the data available to the public at https://www.argoverse.org/av2.html#mapchange-link, along with code and models at https://github.com/johnwlambert/tbv under the the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license.
abstract_zh: 高清 (HD) 地图变化检测是确定传感器数据和地图数据何时由于现实世界的变化而不再一致的任务。我们通过从超过 9 个月的自动驾驶车队运营中挖掘数千小时的数据，收集了该任务的第一个数据集，并将其命名为“信任但验证”(TbV) 数据集。我们提出了基于学习的公式来解决鸟瞰和自我视图中的问题。由于真实地图变化很少，而且矢量图很容易综合操作，因此我们依靠模拟数据来训练我们的模型。也许令人惊讶的是，我们证明此类模型可以推广到现实世界的分布。该数据集由在北美六个城市收集的地图和日志组成，是迄今为止最大的 AV 数据集之一，拥有超过 780 万张图像。我们根据 CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 许可证在 https://www.argoverse.org/av2.html#mapchange-link 上向公众提供数据，并在 https://github.com/johnwlambert/tbv 上提供代码和模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于跨模态融合的高精度地图变化检测方法，通过分析传感器数据与地图数据的不一致性来识别现实世界中的变化。
AI.motivation: 高精度地图变化检测面临真实场景中变化数据稀缺、矢量地图易被合成操控的挑战，需依赖模拟数据训练模型并验证其在真实场景中的泛化能力。
AI.method: 构建首个TbV数据集（包含780万张图像），采用鸟瞰视角和自车视角的深度学习方法，结合跨模态融合技术进行地图变化检测。
AI.result: 模型在模拟数据训练后成功泛化至真实场景，数据集涵盖北美六座城市，提供公开的代码和模型（CC BY-NC-SA 4.0许可）。
AI.conclusion: 跨模态融合方法有效解决了地图变化检测问题，TbV数据集为自动驾驶领域提供了重要的研究资源。
